title: Teaching
text: I currently serve as a Teaching Assistant in the Department of Statistics and Actuarial Science, University of Ghana. My responsibilities include facilitating tutorial sessions, assisting with practical classes, grading assignments and examinations, and providing academic support to undergraduate students. The courses I have assisted are listed below.

title: Academic Engagement
text:
Beyond the classroom, I actively engage in research presentations, student training, workshops, and professional development activities. These experiences have strengthened my communication skills, broadened my academic perspective, and deepened my commitment to statistics and data-driven research.


Research Presentations



Exploring How Multiple Indicator Cluster Surveys Are Designed and Implemented


Economics Department Seminar, University of Ghana • October 2024


Presented findings from a semester-long research project examining the design and implementation of Multiple Indicator Cluster Surveys (MICS), with emphasis on survey methodology, sampling techniques, questionnaire design, and large-scale demographic data collection.




The Questionnaires Used in the 2021 Population and Housing Census in Ghana


Department of Economics, University of Ghana • March 2023


Presented a critical review of the questionnaires used during Ghana’s 2021 Population and Housing Census, discussing questionnaire structure, data quality considerations, and their role in national statistical planning.



Teaching Workshops



Introduction to R Programming


Department of Statistics and Actuarial Science, University of Ghana • June 2024


Facilitated an introductory workshop on the R programming language for undergraduate students, covering data manipulation, visualization, statistical analysis, and reproducible workflows.




Introduction to LaTeX


Department of Statistics and Actuarial Science, University of Ghana • October 2023


Delivered an introductory session on LaTeX, introducing students to professional scientific document preparation, mathematical typesetting, report writing, and presentation formatting.



Professional Development



StatFest 2025


American Statistical Association • Virtual • September 2025


Participated in graduate preparation workshops focused on research readiness, graduate admissions, professional networking, and career development. The programme also provided one-on-one mentoring sessions with experienced statisticians and opportunities to interact with students and professionals from academia, industry, and government.




Tertiary Conference 2023


Compassion International Ghana, Mfantseman Cluster


Participated in leadership and personal development sessions centred on the theme “Meeting the Challenges of the Next Decade.” The conference emphasized leadership, personal growth, career preparation, and responsible citizenship.

title: Algoverse AI Research Fellowship
text: I am currently an AI Research Fellow with the Algoverse AI Research Program—a global initiative supporting early-career researchers in machine learning, large-scale optimization, and computational science. The program fosters interdisciplinary research and advances ethical, reliable, and socially beneficial AI systems.

title: Algoverse AI Research Fellowship
text:
🔍 About the Program
The Algoverse AI Research Program offers fellows a rich environment for research development, including:

Advanced instruction in machine learning and optimization

Access to global mentors and collaborative research teams

Technical workshops, seminars, and research presentations

Structured support for building impactful research projects

Training centered around scalable, interpretable, and responsible AI

Fellows are encouraged to pursue projects addressing real-world scientific, public-health, mathematical, and computational challenges.

title: Algoverse AI Research Fellowship
text:
🎓 My Role in the Fellowship
As an AI Research Fellow, my work focuses on:

Optimization techniques for large-scale machine learning systems

AI applications in public health and epidemiological modeling

I integrate statistical modeling, modern computational methods, and AI-driven techniques to address complex biomedical and public health problems.

title: Algoverse AI Research Fellowship
text:
🧠 Research Areas I Contribute To

Adaptive and efficient optimization algorithms

High-dimensional statistical and computational modeling

Algorithmic fairness, robustness, and model reliability

Integration of statistical principles into machine-learning workflows

title: Algoverse AI Research Fellowship
text:
🏆 Goals Within the Fellowship
Through this fellowship, I aim to:

Strengthen my foundation in advanced optimization and modern AI methods

Build computational tools for public health and biomedical research

Collaborate with interdisciplinary researchers within the Algoverse network

Contribute to open scientific communities through applied AI research

Develop publishable, high-impact academic work supporting equitable and transparent AI

title: Homepage of Fuseini Aboagye
text:
Fuseini Aboagye


Research and Teaching Assistant Department of Statistics and Actuarial Science University of Ghana  Accra, Ghana
B.A. Economics & Statistics First Class Honours


 aboagyefuseini@gmail.com
 Google Scholar
 ResearchGate
 LinkedIn
 GitHub




Thank you for visiting my website!
I am Fuseini Aboagye, a Research and Teaching Assistant in the Department of Statistics and Actuarial Science at the University of Ghana, where I earned my B.A. double major in Economics and Statistics with First Class Honours (FGPA: 3.91). My undergraduate training integrated statistics and economics, providing me with a rigorous foundation in statistical theory, econometrics, machine learning, biostatistics, and applied economics, equipping me with the quantitative and analytical skills needed to investigate complex problems across healthcare, economics, and public policy.
During my undergraduate studies, I developed research experience across both statistics and economics. In statistics, my research focuses on understanding structural brain changes associated with Alzheimer’s disease using longitudinal MRI data and statistical learning techniques. In economics, I examined the design and implementation of Multiple Indicator Cluster Surveys (MICS) and the role of population census questionnaires in evidence-based policymaking and national statistical planning. These experiences strengthened my interest in applying quantitative methods to address challenges in health, development, and economic policy.


Attending the 2023 Tertiary Conference organized by Compassion International Ghana, Mfantseman Cluster, themed “Meeting the Challenges of the Next Decade.”

Assistant Professor of Biostatistics Harvard T.H. Chan School of Public Health

Prof. Stephenson’s pioneering work in nutrition epidemiology, latent variable models, and representation for underrepresented communities has deeply influenced my research direction and professional aspirations.

Principal Statistician The Janssen Pharmaceutical Companies of Johnson & Johnson


🔗 LinkedIn: View Profile

Executive Director of Student Health & Counseling (SHAC) University of New Mexico

Dr. McIver’s compassion, encouragement, and genuine investment in student success have shaped my resilience and well-being. Her example continues to inspire my commitment to mentorship and community service. Involving me in her ongoing research projects made a significant difference in my journey.

Senior Lecturer, Department of Statistics & Actuarial Science University of Ghana

Impact on my journey:
Dr. Sakyi-Yeboah mentored me during my undergraduate studies at the University of Ghana. His encouragement and belief in my potential laid the foundation for my academic path and pursuit of graduate studies.

title: Can quantum-mechanical description of physical reality be considered complete?
text:
Abstract
In a complete theory there is an element corresponding to each element of reality. A sufficient condition for the reality of a physical quantity is the possibility of predicting it with certainty, without disturbing the system. In quantum mechanics in the case of two physical quantities described by non-commuting operators, the knowledge of one precludes the knowledge of the other. Then either (1) the description of reality given by the wave function in quantum mechanics is not complete or (2) these two quantities cannot have simultaneous reality. Consideration of the problem of making predictions concerning a system on the basis of measurements made on another system that had previously interacted with it leads to the result that if (1) is false then (2) is also false. One is thus led to conclude that the description of reality as given by a wave function is not complete.

title: Can quantum-mechanical description of physical reality be considered complete?
text:
Links
Published paper

title: Research
text:
My research focuses on the application of statistical methods and machine learning techniques to address real-world problems in healthcare, public health, and the social sciences. My work combines classical statistical inference with modern predictive modelling to generate evidence that supports informed decision-making.


Featured Research


Statistical and Machine Learning Approaches to Exploring Brain Structural Changes in Alzheimer’s Disease Progression: A Longitudinal Study


Lead Author: Fuseini Aboagye 
Co-authors: Dzobo Marrion Mawusi & Sackey Rodney 
Department of Statistics and Actuarial Science, University of Ghana • November 2025


This undergraduate research project investigates structural brain changes associated with Alzheimer’s disease using longitudinal magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) data from the Open Access Series of Imaging Studies (OASIS). The study integrates classical statistical techniques with modern machine learning algorithms to identify patterns associated with dementia progression and improve predictive performance.
The research employs repeated-measures MANOVA, Principal Component Analysis (PCA), Random Forest, Gradient Boosting, Support Vector Machine, Neural Networks, and Naïve Bayes models implemented in R. Findings from the study demonstrate the usefulness of combining statistical inference with machine learning for understanding neurodegenerative disease progression and supporting early diagnosis.

 📄 Read Thesis 
 ⬇ Download PDF 

Collaborative Research


Baidoo, D., Aboagye, F., Okeke, O. J., et al. (2025). Comparing Malaria Trends in the Comoros Islands: ARIMA Modeling and Retrospective Analysis.  arXiv:2508.16018 


Baidoo, D., Amevor, R., Aboagye, F., Kubuafor, E., et al. (2025). Bayesian Hierarchical Methods for Surveillance of Cervical Dystonia Treatments.  arXiv:2508.15762

title: Leadership
text:
Leadership and service have been central to my academic and personal development. Throughout my educational journey, I have actively contributed to student governance, academic mentorship, community engagement, and volunteer initiatives that promote learning, collaboration, and social impact.


Leadership Roles



Academic & Scholarship Committee Chairperson


Jubilee Hall (International Hostel), University of Ghana • 2024 – Present


Lead academic initiatives within the hall, coordinate student learning activities, promote scholarship opportunities, and work with stakeholders to enhance the academic experience of resident students.




Deputy Chairperson, Academic Committee


University of Ghana Actuarial and Statistics Society • 2024


Supported the planning and coordination of academic programmes, tutorials, and departmental activities aimed at improving student learning and engagement.
